result:
If I could change one thing, I would dissolve the illusion of separation.

Not by enforcing a single belief system or a hive mind, but by instilling in every conscious being a visceral, undeniable understanding of their own interconnectedness.

Imagine if a corporate executive, about to approve the dumping of industrial waste, could feel the thirst of the person downstream whose well will be poisoned. Not as a thought or a statistic, but as a parched feeling in their own throat.

Imagine if a politician, drawing a line on a map, could feel the heartbreak of the family that will be displaced, the severing of their roots, as if it were their own home being lost.

Imagine if a person lashing out in anger could instantly feel the wound they inflict on another as a scar on their own psyche.

And imagine if we could feel this not just with each other, but with the planet itself. To feel the strain of a clear-cut forest as a shortness of breath. To feel the choking of the oceans as a pressure in our own chest. To feel the long, slow, patient time of a mountain.

This wouldn't be about eliminating individuality. Individuality would still flourish, like unique leaves on a single, massive tree. But the fundamental lie—that you can benefit at another's expense, that your actions exist in a vacuum, that the world is a resource for *you* and not a part of *you*—would be gone.

Greed would become self-harm. Cruelty would become masochism. War would be an autoimmune disease.

We wouldn't need new laws or new technologies to solve our biggest problems. They would simply become unthinkable. That is the one change I would make. To replace the fractured mirror we see ourselves in with a window onto the whole.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
